33.reason: The condition uses <= which sorts in ascending order, but the problem requires descending order of exponents. Should be >=.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  The condition uses <= which sorts in ascending order, but the problem requires descending order of exponents. Should be >=.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  The condition should be `h2[j] < h2[j+1]` to sort in descending order; using `<=` makes the bubble sort unstable and may cause incorrect ordering when exponents are equal.

52.reason: [After merging, i should be decremented to recheck the current index because the next exponent has shifted into position i+1. Without i--, the loop skips the new element, potentially missing further merges.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [After merging, i should be decremented to recheck the current index because the next exponent has shifted into position i+1. Without i--, the loop skips the new element, potentially missing further merges.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  // need to decrement i to recheck the same index after shifting
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing i-- after decrementing k causes the loop to skip checking the newly shifted element against the current one

56.reason: [Should only print if h1[i]!=0, otherwise print zero coefficient terms. The problem expects only non-zero terms in output.]

45.reason: The loop increments i after merging, skipping re-evaluation of the current index. This fails to correctly merge when more than two terms share the same exponent. Additionally, it does not handle removal of terms whose coefficient becomes zero after addition.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The loop increments i after merging, skipping re-evaluation of the current index. This fails to correctly merge when more than two terms share the same exponent. Additionally, it does not handle removal of terms whose coefficient becomes zero after addition.
